selectedTitle: WeWork's Fatal Decisions: A Financial Autopsy
thumbnailPrompt: Cinematic scene dramatization, 16:9 aspect ratio. A dramatic wide-angle interior shot of a WeWork co-working space in visible decay and collapse — overturned mid-century modern chairs, cracked exposed-brick walls with peeling motivational murals reading fragments of hustle culture slogans, flickering Edison bulb pendant lights casting deep amber shadows across a dusty open floor. In the LEFT third of the frame, a lone figure in a rumpled dark blazer stands with their back to camera, head slightly bowed, staring down at a shattered glass conference table covered in scattered financial documents and red-ink balance sheets. The figure is dramatically lit from above-left by a single shaft of cold industrial light cutting through a grimy skylight, creating strong tonal separation between the illuminated subject and the murky collapsed office behind them. The RIGHT third of the frame is clean negative space — a softly blurred, smoke-hazy beige wall with minimal detail, reserved for text overlay. No critical elements in the bottom-right corner. High contrast chiaroscuro lighting: warm amber decay in the background, cool clinical spotlight on the figure. Shallow depth of field. Film grain texture. Cinematic color grade with desaturated greens and crushed blacks evoking institutional failure and hubris. Shot on anamorphic lens with slight lens flare from the overhead light source.
